top_profane.text:
I spent DAYS, literally - FULL days, looking for something like this. I have SO many little organizers everywhere - for both beads & charms. I thought this was my answer for consolidating the beads at least. I was so excited I think I even paid for express shipping.
Then I opened it.😳😲😠🤬😤
1st pic shows what I was presented with.😠
2nd pic shows why.🤬
3rd pis shows just pure laziness.🤦‍♀️
4th pic shows a possible option is actually a failure.😢😤🤬
It all comes down to the foam used. It's cheap af crap. Why any effort is made to make it in the 1st place is beyond me. There is zero use for it, it's so cheap.
I have a battery holder case. THAT thing has the foam quality I was expecting, just to give a reference. This stuff? I wouldn't even use it to line a shipping box. I can't think of ANY use for this - other than to spit in the faces of Americans.
Then, it's not even cut properly for the case. It's *just* a little off. Enough to be annoying. And remind you just how cheap it is.
Finally, given the cheapness of the foam, you'd think they'd at least glue it to some kind of cardboard bottom or something to support it. Like, so when you pull off the top 2nd layer of smaller jars, it remains stable & they don't just plop out? While EMPTY even!
But no, see 2nd pic.
I have NO idea just how they expect this to be properly & satisfactorily utilized. The jars on the top layer - the lids don't have lips that may help it at least catch on the edge of the cheap foam, like hanging from a ledge by your fingernails.
There is a equally cheap, zippered flap inside the case that would make you think - well maybe they expect ppl to put the top layer in there, thus separating the 2 & providing support on the bottom. If so why didn't other shipped like that?
Cause it doesn't even come close to fitting in there, that's why. (See pic 4).
See, now I am crying. I'm SO desperate to get all this crap organized & thought THIS was my answer. I bought 1 to test my theory & see what all I could use it for. I would have bought 2-3 or more if it worked out.
But because they couldn't be bothered to either use good, quality foam, &/or support the bottom of each layer, choosing instead to insult their customers - crushingly it's not close to being a viable option.
I know it's stupid that I'm crying over foam 🙄, but I REALLY need a system/way to organize all the stuff I have. They have the key here! This is a GREAT idea. If PROPERLY built, this would likely be the next big money boom for them & a must have for a number of crafting groups, just to start with!
But they chose to insult their customers instead & put it together with subpar quality materials. Even the jars are cheap. I can tell by holding them I could probably crush it with my weakest hand, easily. The lining is...I don't know how to eloquently describe it. It's like crunchy plastic. Like a dollar store shower curtain? Only worse. I could probably tear it with just my pinkies, or pop a hole in it with a dull fingernail. The pattern chosen for the liner is from the homeless junk pile it's so ugly. (No offense to the homeless) The zippers are just begging to get jammed on nothing.
The exterior case seems ok so far. Obviously very cheap, but not ready to fail just because you looked at it. So far at least. I haven't really examined it.
I'm fully aware that most of the crap sold here is cheap crap from china. I have bought a ton of stuff & I'm not blind or stupid. But this? This is just beyond the pale. That they took a great idea & pooped all over it? Then turned around & sold it? It's ridiculously beyond insulting & disappointing & no words for how disrespecting.
I spent the ENTIRE week searching for something to help organize my stuff (thank you OCD). 100+ hrs. So imagine my giddiness when I found what seemed like the absolutely perfect item to begin with? 💃💃💃💃
Then it's completely ruined due to cheap af foam? And it's the ONLY one out there like this, that has jars the right size for your needs?
I keep flipping btwn furious & sad crying.
I'm just beside myself. When I found this I thought "OMG! PERFECT!" *jumps while clapping* SO damn excited was I! I can't recall the last time I was SO excited over an item, thinking it would make my life a little easier.
Now? I'm just crying I'm so disappointed & angry & disgusted & insulted by the lack of quality control. My brain letting unkind thoughts out their cages to bounce around my head.
I could have put up with the rest of the pitiful quality parts. We're sadly stuck with China & their crap, so I'm used to their crap. But there was usually at least 1 thing that was decent enough quality that helped one to put up with the rest of the pathetic level quality parts. But the foam? The foam is the key, the glue, that allows you to ignore the crap overall quality of the product. You CAN'T chince on the freaking foam. It's the heart of the dang thing.
It's like selling a new car with a decade old, WELL used engine in it.
If I can find someone with a laser cutter, I'll buy my own materials - from USA companies - using GOOD foam that will be glued to a bottom, & make these myself. Thanks for the idea! 🍆😶's

Beautiful strand, great color range throughout the stones, strung on fish line so held up great, n came on time n packaged well. Totally 5 stars n would recommend and buy more myself any day. Great strand of chips for Larimer.

So let me brief people on chips because I often see people complain about chip strands n seem to think they should get a strand of rich A grade stones n that's NOT what chip strands are. A hank of chip strands can even vary greatly. Size can vary greatly and chip strands are NOT nugget strands. Typically sold in small, medium, or large, n once in a while extra small being 2-3 mm or smaller. Small run about 3-4 mm, medium 4- 5 sometimes 6, n large can range about 6-8 but sometimes as low as 5mm also. There's a range in sizes that usually ranges about 3 sizes of mm in chips ROUGHLY. Because chips are just that, chips. Anything larger is classified nuggets NOT chips. Often times people do closeup pictures so you can see the chips better assuming the people who buy chips already know what they are doing n buying when they don't n then they complain cuz they didn't get nuggets. Lol.

The other thing is grade. Chips are NOT A-grade stones unless they state they are A-grade chips n rest assured you will NOT get a strand of A-grade Larimer chips for $10. The whole point n beauty of chips is that you DO GET a whole range n spectrum of color from the entire crystals or stones. NOT a single color. Stones like Pipestone n jasper, black obsidian chips, etc, that are rocks generally have a very uniform solid stone color in chips throughout. But crystals n stones like turquoise lapis Larimer NEVER do.

Stones like Carnelian you can get a deep red-orange to milky white n clear, n same with amethyst, rose qrtz, or any crystal type stone n often times you may just medium orange to the lighter range with very little of it in its deepest color. To get solid uniform color as in rich orange red you must seek out A grade. Larimer is the same way if you want the deep blue color. These are chips NOT A grade so you should get a good mixed color range from the entire stone n can EXPECT only small of the deepest color range if any. THAT IS what standard chips ARE and about.

Chips also, due to the irregularity of the stones have all kinds of awkward angled drill holes as well. Difficult to drill due to the odd shapes n often have holes off center and along the edge of the stone. One can tell that and know that just by looking at the pictures of any chip strands. That IS how chips are n meant to be. They are very irregular stones that come from scraps leftover from cutting the GRADE A areas out of stones n crystals. They are NOT A-grade stones unless stated as such n you can expect to pay a pretty penny for A-grade also. You must also pay attention when using chips as well because often if the drill hole is very close to the edge n you string or cinch them to tightly you can and will break the stone.

What makes chips so beautiful IS the wide range of colors you DO get, and they can be used in a variety of ways n projects or just like they are for bracelets n necklaces etc. You can ALWAYS expect to have some that break n some waste. It IS the nature of buying chips which are basically the scraps that are tumbled, polished, n drilled so as not to waste the precious stone scraps which are beautiful in and of themselves in a different way than actual A-grade stones. That's why they are sold so cheap. Because they are stone scraps. They don't break up choice grade A crystals or rock to make chips, they are the scraps left over from cutting out grade A sections of stones n crystals for fine or designer grade jewelry pieces. To expect uniform grade A stones all of high color n uniform color you must seek out GRADE A chips. Expect to pay a high price for them also. You will NOT get a strand of chips that is uniform in color unless it is certain stones. Sometimes you can get something like amethyst or rose qtz pretty uniform at B grade but if there is NO GRADE listed then you MUST understand it is a crap shoot what you will get and you MUST EXPECT a huge color range n most likely NOT a lot of high grade color. That just IS HOW IT IS. The nature of chips. They ARE scrap stone made in to rugged irregular rough beads n to expect more is irrational. If you want high quality color in A grade you MUST seek out A grade chips n a stone like Larimer you'll probably pay more like $50-$100 depending on how high the quality n the size and price will be simular to what you will pay for cut stones. There's very little price break for A grade chips than for cut stones or beads n simply priced much like cut the cut beads are priced.

The other thing people need to figure out is turquoise is almost ALWAYS imitation or synthetic unless stated otherwise. In chip mixes with real gemstones turquoise is seldom real because it has been mined out n super expensive now for real. When you get real in chips you MUST expect a good portion of black to brown stones also. You will NOT get a strand of solid turquoise colored chips unless again, you seek out A grade REAL turquoise. Expect to pay a pretty penny as well. Most the kits n mixes of genuine stones the turquoise is dyed howlite or synthetic unless they state it is REAL. Even most REAL turquoise is DYED also as are many stones n crystals. Dyed, heatreated, bleached, stabilized etc... A very common practice because so much of the quality A grade stones have been mined out. The geometries n properties of turquoise or say rose qtz are in the stone n a true rose qtz stone but the color enhanced with dye to give it more color cuz the choice stones are hard to find now n you will pay premium high prices for a rich pink natural piece of rose qtz. NOT dyed. This is true for all stones n has to do with availability of various stones and how common they are worldwide to be found.

Something like Larimer is only found one place in the world and relatively new stone compared to stones like Lapis, Turquoise, or Carnelian which have been around for centuries n even millenia for some. So it runs higher than a lot of stones n to get A grade anything you must expect to pay the price. You must also pay attention what they state also n that seldom when ordering chips will you get what you see in the pictures. You MUST understand that chips are a crap shoot when ordering by mail or online. Pay attention to size, n grade, or what they state n read the descriptions on them farther down. The less thorough description the greater the crap shoot you are taking. Understand n know how stones are handled and what the differences are n understand that most stones today ARE DYED n treated in some way. Understand when it says opal, or opalite, you ARE getting qtz crystals or glass that has been treated with an opalized finish. NOT REAL OPALS. REAL opals even a small chip are off the charts expensive n couldn't even be mined for years. Especially fire opals cuz the mine caved in. Opals like Mexican opals is usually REAL n not dyed at all due to the abundance of it n relatively cheap in terms of semiprecious stones.

I'm in my 60s n been working with gemstones n beads since 2nd grade and believe me, it's almost impossible to get high quality grade A anything without paying a high price today. Coral, malachite, n lapis are 3 other stones that are very expensive now for real natural gradeA quality n most are dyed n treated. Mankind has raped the earth dry of most quality stones so coral is almost ALL dyed n you can't even get colors like blue or purple anymore n white is often times bleached. Lapis to is almost always dyed n very expensive now to get choice grade A lapis n malachite also. So often I see reviews n people think if a stone looks dyed it is not real n that's just not true. The truth is that many natural REAL stones ARE DYED n treated in all kinds of ways because so much of the quality stones are long gone. Or very expensive n if you don't know stones, crystals, n gems perhaps you should read up on them and learn more before writing reviews because I often see reviews n people complain NOT because they didn't get what they thought they would but because they lack knowledge of gemstones n crystals period n want A grade stuff for $5 or something n have no clue what is what n how it works. People selling do n assume those buying know the ropes also n how you gage things n that most turquoise nowadays IS DYED and/or synthetic n so on. They do their descriptions based on standard knowledge that those in the field know n get n understand NOT for the average person. Most people on here do leave good descriptions based on the standard ways of identification its the buyers that have no clue what means what or how to gage n what to expect. I keep seeing people complain when if they knew what means what they wouldn't have a complaint at all n got exactly what was stated. Or if the knew beads n gemstones, rocks, n crystals but they dont.

Take some time n learn about stones n crystals as well as sales terminology that gem n stone sellers use n what is what before you purchase. Understand that things like chips are just that, chips. They are very irregular stones that range widely in size n shape n the difference between sizes n size ranges n even the difference between nuggets, chips, n pebbles. You want a stone that is chip like with very centered holes then look for pebbles. NOT chips. If you want large stones look for nuggets.

When using chips in jewelry be selective n try to use ones that are thicker with relatively centered holes so they aren't breaking n save the risker ones for projects where the stones don't suffer a lot of wear n tear like earrings or pouches n clothing or on art projects. There are many ways one can use chips n the fragile ones can be used in meditation as well. Understand n expect that chips even on one strand come in many sizes n thicknesses n if you want thick n stable n good size then look for ones that state they are large chips or go for pebbles NOT chips. Typically chips when just listed as such n not sized or stated then dont set yourself for heartbreak no matter what the picture looks like. Understand when not stated you are most likely getting chips 4-5 mm if not smaller n more waste than average. They simply do close up shots so you can see the chips better n you can expect a wide range of color n stones like lapis, turquoise etc you will most likely be getting a good amount of black to brown chips n even white or very light blue with lapis and/or expect them to be dyed. Often looking more like sodalite than lapis when natural and undyed. Or they WILL BE DYED chips if they are real n have good color but the stone geometries n properties are still there just not of good color so they dye them.

Know your stones, know what's most available in stones and the world, are they rocks or crystals in make up because it all makes a difference n how availed any stone is. True opals is very rare to get so opalite was cheaper to simulate opals n good quality opalite is done using qtz crystal n cheaper is done using glass. Things like jasper n marble are usually natural n true to color n not died n the strands very uniform in color n have good natural color. Crystals NOT. Stones like lapis, turquoise, n malachite almost always dyed n heat-treated or imitation n often created from howlite which is a white stone found in abundance all over the world. One of the most common stones used for making imitation stones IS howlite. Many crystals are now dyed also n stones like pineapple or strawberry n cherry crystal are nothing but quartz crystal that has been dyed. Not to fool people but because they can n it makes for some new n intereeting n different colors using crystals. Many blue colored crystals are dyed also n even certain agates. Not done to decieve people but because of the loss/lack of good quality stones n demand for stone's so they color enhance the lighter stones. People that work in the field be it bead shops designers n jewelry makers know all this stuff n how things are measured, graded, the many things done to treat stones etc. The average human doesn't n then gets on here buying crystal chips n gettin mad they didn't get diamond nuggets for $5. Lol. Ya gotta know what your doin n buying n be reading what they say or dont say n understand that most pictures are closeups n don't reflect actual size the description below is what you need to be reading.

If you look at kits with 30 or 40 stone types read what they are. Many will say the turquoise and/or lapis is imitation or it says opalite or opal which is considered common knowledge it is opalized qtz crystal in the field n not real opal. Or they will state opalized glass if it's done on glass n not qtz crystal. Turquoise will say imitation in mixed gem kits if it is as will lapis n malachite if nort then expect the real to have its share of black, brown, n white for lapis in the mixes n not a lot of good solid colored stones.

OK, I'm over it. I just thought I would point somethings out about chips n stones because I keep finding great humor n lack of knowledge in reviews on chips n mixed stone kits especially and the bulk of the negative reviews are clearly from people who have no clue anything about gems n stones n chips or what is what. Chips are the cheapest gems n stones you can get because they are made from scraps left over from the cutting of stones. To find one or two diamonds in the mix is bonus ONLY. It is NOT a given to get A grade stones at all when buying chips it is bonus you n your lucky day.

This Larimer strand of chips I recieved is very NICE CHOICE strand of chips and medium to large even in size has a beautiful range of color n zero waste although once I open it I am sure to find some stones of risk drilled closely to the edge because that's just how it is and I expect it. Just by looking at it I can see some but I examine the entire strand n looks to be very few of even those. That is most common to happen in very thin n small stones or when the hole is in a very thin spot of the chip and can NOT be helped. They're chips. No big deal good luck finding a chip strand of any kind that does not have such stones in it. Know it, expect it, plan for it when buying chips, n buy extra if you need a certain amount because that is just the nature of chips n impossible to get them any other way. You must buy pebbles rather than chips if you want centered drilled stones with no breakage. The Beaty of chips IS their irregularity and huge color range giving one a wide variety of stones to work with both in color n size in what nakes them chips n what they are. This is definitely a choice strand and I'm thrilled because I got it to make me jewelry with. I always buy one when I don't know a company to see what I get FIRST and if it meets the description and packed with care that says a lot. To get a choice strand is even more the bonus because I know chips n have all kinds. I love just wearing strands of chips like they are because I love color range n irregularity of the stones and it' offerss a different texture than uniform stones. I've seen some not so good looking strands before also. These didn't just pass the test as stated but I expected I'd recieve a strand with more waste to it. It's rare I buy them from a place I don't know or know how they grade and view chips n never on Amazon before so I was a little leary because chips are a crap shoot. I am thrilled with the choice strand I recieved n plan to get more n would definitely recommend them to anyone. I will also be happy to give a review if I buy others or more as well. Because I know chips are a crap shoot n people need to understand n expect to get strands that will have some waste or fragile chips n that it is just the nature of chips. Count the great choice strands you get as bonus n scored or hit the jackpot cuz chips are a crap shoot if you can't hand pick them n don't sweat the small stuff. This is a choice strand, couldn't have picked from a bunch better myself n I'm very happy n pleased with my purchase. Greatly appreciated n looking forward to more.

Hope this helps those of you new n learning to help you better understand chips n chip strands n how to evaluate better n better what to expect. They are made from stone scraps NOT choice grade A cuts. Pay attention to the descriptions n what they say n don't always go for the cheapest. I looked at many n read all the descriptions thoroughly because I know the crap shoot chips are. Sometimes I'll go cheap if I don't need a whole lot but I was lookin for a pretty sound 32" at least n actually more than that so I chose these. They had a good description, good picture with a good average strand n its important to picture a good average strand also n not an exaggerated strand or even poor strand but an average strand in terms of chips. These are very nice n got better than I expected, a wonderful strand even n can't wait to make them up into something for myself, n the price was great also for the value n such great usability with little to no waste. Great deal. I am delighted with my purchase. Thank you very much I look forward to more.

Opps, almost forgot, I recieved a bonus gift also of 2 very beautiful round tiger eye beads with my order. Perfect for a pair of earrings and very pretty. That was sweet also. Throw beads. I'll take anything for free n make something with it. Thank you again. Lol.

top_profane.text:
Welcome to 8th part of CLONE MADNESS dear readers, where we once again take a look at THE CLONE SAGA, which has now officially begun its derailment from this point onward!

Well folks, we’re finally getting into the stories that most readers immediately think of when talking about how bad THE CLONE SAGA was. Remember how in my review of THE MARK OF KAINE, I stated that while the narrative structure and dialogue were a mess, I was still able to enjoy the comic because of several enjoyable scenes. It was for that reason that I considered THE MARK OF KAINE to fall into the category of being a “guilty-pleasure.” I was a little hesitant about calling the book that at first, as while I genuinely enjoyed parts of the story, there were points that did manage to heavily frustrate me. However, after reading THE TRIAL OF PETER PARKER I can safely justify THE MARK OF KAINE’S status as a guilty pleasure, as that story at least had several great scenes and concepts despite its narrative shortcomings. This one on the other hand, has an even worse narrative structure, and very few good scenes in it at all. This is undoubtedly one of the most infamous, horribly written, and controversial story lines from THE CLONE SAGA (second only to the one came right after, MAXIMUM CLONAGE). I’ll admit that I had prior knowledge about what occurred in this story arc from other reviewers, but even their critiques were not enough to prepare me for how bad THE TRIAL OF PETER PARKER is. But before I tear this comic apart, I need to provide some backstory. As I stated in my reviews of THE MARK OF KAINE and THE LOST YEARS, Peter Parker was arrested under murder charges by Detective Jacob Raven from Salt Lake City. In reality though, the murders were actually committed by Kaine, whose first victim was Louise Kennedy, Raven’s partner and Kaine’s lover, who he killed for betraying him at the end of THE LOST YEARS. During the events of THE MARK OF KAINE, Peter was able to escape from prison and Ben Reilly offered to take Peter’s place so that he could be with his pregnant wife while also searching for evidence of the real killer. In the two-part story arc CROSSFIRE, Detective Raven provided Peter evidence that Kaine was the actual murderer in the form of “the Mark of Kaine” scarred across his face, as that same mark was also found on the bodies of the murder victims, along with Peter’s fingerprints. With Ben in prison awaiting trial, Kaine still on the loose with Spider-Man and Raven hunting him down, it has all led up to THE TRIAL OF PETER PARKER, which is one of the most important stories in the entire CLONE SAGA. This was the story that not only promised the resolution of the murder mystery subplot, but also the reveal of Kaine’s true identity, and answer to who was the real Spider-Man and who was the clone: Peter or Ben? Unfortunately though, while THE TRIAL OF PETER PARKER ended up delivering what it promised to the readers, the execution of it was such a failure of global proportions that just leaves me speechless…

The story opens with Spidey swinging across New York while the murder trial has just started. While Ben is filling in for Peter, Peter is attempting to find Kaine and force him to admit that he’s the actual murderer. Kaine suddenly attacks Peter out of nowhere (I’m not even kidding with that), and openly refuses to admit his crimes to the court. Because Ben Reilly has taken Peter’s place in the murder trial, Kaine sees this as the perfect opportunity to have his revenge against Reilly if he is given the death penalty. But the fight is interrupted by the sudden appearance of Judas Traveller. Remember Judas Traveller? He was that boring, underdeveloped god-like villain from THE CLONE SAGA’s opening arc, POWER AND RESPONSIBILITY. The boring villain who nearly ruined the story that officially marked Ben’s return to the Spider-Man mythos (someone please shoot me now…). Traveller then teleports Peter and Kaine away to Ravencroft Asylum, where he plans to hold a trial of his own for Peter. The charges in this case, are that Peter is somehow responsible for the creation of all the asylum’s inmates, which include Carnage, Shriek, Carrion, Vermin, and various others. With Ben facing the threat of being sentenced to death in a badly written trial sequence, Traveller prosecuting Peter with his pseudo-bullcrap arguments, and Kaine refusing to save Ben Reilly, will Peter and Ben be given the verdicts they desire, or will they both be handed down the ultimate judgments? Either way, Traveller will continue to waste readers’ time, Kaine’s identity will finally be revealed, and the question everyone has been asking since the beginning of THE CLONE SAGA will be finally answered: Who is the original Spider-Man and who is the clone? Peter Parker or Ben Reilly?

I’m just amazed at how horribly written THE TRIAL OF PETER PARKER is. This is the first CLONE SAGA story since SMOKE AND MIRRORS that was enough to anger and frustrate me, though admittedly for different reasons than main problem most people have with it. With the exception of MAXIMUM CLONAGE, this story is quite easily the most infamous of the CLONE SAGA as its last chapter (MAJOR SPOILER ALERT) revealed that Peter was actually the clone, and Ben was the original. This plot twist angered a lot of fans, but in my opinion however, it’s the least of THE TRIAL OF PETER PARKER’s failures. Among this comic’s numerous flaws, the ones that were the most prominent to me were the padded out narrative structure, redundant dialogue, the idiotic scenes with Judas Traveller, as well as what are quite easily the worst courtroom scenes ever written in a fictional story.

While I complained about the narrative structure in THE MARK OF KAINE being a mess, it’s honestly worse here. The story literally opens with Kaine showing up out of nowhere and fighting Peter, then Judas Traveller abducts Peter and Kaine to conducts his own trial, the two escape Traveller, Peter drags Kaine to the courthouse, Kaine reveals his identity as a failed Spider-Man clone and admits to the court that he's the actual murderer before being arrested, and then the story ends with the reveal that Ben is the actually not the clone. Because of the horrible plot construction, there are very few scenes of the actual murder trial. Over half the story is just fight scenes between Spider-Man and Kaine, which are padded out to the point where they fill up the majority of the issues (especially the first and third). Because of the padding and lack of pacing, the dialogue is also badly handled, lacking any weight or depth. It eventually becomes extremely repetitive when Peter and Kaine repeat the same things over and over again to pad out the issues. It’s literally Kaine saying, “I hate Ben Reilly but will do whatever I can to protect you Peter,” and Peter replying, “Ben is a noble person and I’ll drag you to that courthouse and beat a confession out of you,” over and over again. I haven’t seen such horrendous padding since HOLY TERROR and THE DRACO.

Speaking of padding, that’s basically all that Judas Traveller and his bogus trial serves to the plot. I already ranted about Traveller in my POWER AND RESPONSIBILITY review, and how he was an underdeveloped antagonist with no defined backstory or purpose in the overall CLONE SAGA narrative. While those are major problems with this character, the worst sin about him in my opinion is that he is unbelievably boring. He's unreadable levels of boring! That’s my main problem with Judas Traveller, as any scene featuring him immediately takes me out of the story and causes me to lose any interest I had whatsoever. As a result, the entire second issue, which features his pseudo-trial in Ravencroft, is unbearably boring, contributing nothing to the plot and wastes the reader’s time with Traveller’s bullcrap rambling. The other major complaint that I have with Traveller’s scenes is that aside from being boring, they go as far to rip-off the BATMAN: THE ANIMATED SERIES episode, THE TRIAL. That episode featured all of Batman’s villains kidnapping the dark knight and placing him on trial for supposedly being responsible for their creation. Like that episode of BATMAN, Traveller’s trial is based around the same charges, that Spider-Man is responsible for the existence of his enemies, and the prosecution is being carried out by his enemies. To even fathom the fact that this comic blatantly rips-off one of the greatest episodes from the animated Batman TV-series, and also doesn’t even bother to at least make it entertaining (similar to my problems with SPIDER-MAN: REIGN) is incredibly insulting to me. Going back to trials though, the worst sin of THE TRIAL OF PETER PARKER in my opinion is that the titular trial is not only given so little focus, but is quite possibly the most badly-written courtroom scene I’ve ever come across in a fictional story. The majority of the trial scenes that we do get are focused on the district attorney, defense lawyer, and jury outright stating the functions of their jobs. This came across as insulting to the reader’s intelligence in my opinion as the writers treat us as if we had never been to an actual trial, or at least watched or read about one in a fictional story. What makes it worse is it comes across as if he writers themselves also don’t know how murder trials work, as the lawyers in this book do things like use insulting language towards the defendant, as well as forcing MJ to testify against her husband when she suposed to be protected by spousal privilege. Never in my entire life, have I seen a courtroom scene so badly botched in a story. But as I stated previously, the issues I've ranted about are not the main reason why this story was so widely hated by Spider-Man fans when it was first released.

But before I delve into what specifically pissed people off about THE TRIAL OF PETER PARKER, I want to elaborate on the only two scenes that I actually enjoyed in this travesty, both of which involve Kaine, who is my other favorite CLONE SAGA character besides Ben Reilly. The first was the scene where Judas Traveller is about to begin his pseudo-trial, but Kaine isn't taking any of Traveller's crap and immediately attacks him, trying to scar him with his "Mark of Kaine." Even though Kaine ends up failing to kill Traveller, it just brings a smile to my face seeing an underrated character that I love trying to kill a crappy character who shouldn't even be in the comic! The other scene that I legitimately thought was good was when Kaine finally reveals to Peter his true identity as a failed Spider-Man clone. As I’ve stated in previous CLONE SAGA reviews, I really love this aspect about Kaine’s character, and felt that it was executed well both here and in the overall narrative of THE CLONE SAGA. While Kaine still does suffer problems in these stories (as does every character in the saga at one point or another), I felt that he actually had much more planning and development than the other villains introduced during the saga. Unlike Judas Traveller or Scrier, who were shoved into the story without any established backstory or purpose, the writers actually took the time into developing Kaine’s character and backstory. I know this not only from what I’ve read about from the writer’s perspective in THE LIFE OF REILLY online essay, but also because the reveal of Kaine being another clone actually fit within the context of THE CLONE SAGA narrative. If you actually look back at earlier stories, all of Kaine’s dialogue in those comics fit within the context of this revelation. Other factors such as Kaine’s hatred of Ben Reilly, and the lengths that he goes to protect Peter and MJ in those stories also help build up to him being a failed clone. Spider-Man editor Glenn Greenberg also stated in THE LIFE OF REILLY that his “Mark of Kaine” scar was the result of Kaine being able to peel flesh off people’s faces with his wall-crawling ability, and that his precognitive visions of the future are an unstable version of the spider-sense. What I also loved about Kaine in this story is that his motivation for him wanting to protect Peter is the result of his belief that Peter is actually the clone. Because of this, Kaine wants to ensure that Peter has a chance at living a happy life whereas he was denied it due to his clone degeneration. I felt that this added a layer of depth and sympathy to Kaine’s character. This also contributed to a great moment where Peter is about to reveal his secret identity to the court in order to protect Reilly from being executed, but Kaine instead confesses his crimes in order to protect Peter’s identity. It was a touching moment that conveyed Kaine having a bit of Peter’s nobility left despite becoming a serial killer.

On a side note about Kaine, a plot hole Linkara that pointed out in his review of this comic was that Kaine's method of framing Reilly for murder was by leaving his fingerprints (which were identical to Ben's) on Louise Kennedy's body. Linkara pointed out however that since Ben was a clone of Peter, how did Kaine not see the risk of Peter accidentally taking the blame? In fairness though, I can defend this particular plot point as it's shown that when Kaine killed Louise back in THE LOST YEARS, Ben was not only there at the scene of the crime after Kaine fled, but that Detective Raven found Ben standing over Louise's body. We also saw in that story that Raven and Ben did know each other before all of this went down, and that Raven had no knowledge of Peter's existence until the events of the present day. The only other murders Kaine committed that caught Detective Raven's attention in the story were the mobsters he killed in THE EXILE RETURNS (as he used his signature "Mark of Kaine" on them, which he also left on Kennedy), which lead him to coming to New York where he was able to gain compare Peter's fingerprints to the ones that Kaine left on Louise Kennedy's body back in Salt Lake City, and since New York was the only state that had records of Peter's prints, it was only then that Raven was able to file a warrant for Peter's arrest. Based on this information, I feel that Kaine believed that Peter was safe from harm as long as he kept Ben away from New York since Raven had no jurisdiction there (until Kaine killed the aforementioned mobsters that is), but Kaine's plans were botched when he followed Ben to the big apple and then got carried away by actively killing Spider-Man villains out of desperation due to the visions he had of MJ being killed by an unknown assailant. Despite that plot hole, the reveal of Kaine’s identity is the only saving grace about this mess of a story in my eyes (that and it ultimately did end up leading to the creation of THE LOST YEARS, which is my number one favorite CLONE SAGA story).

And now it’s finally time to discuss the elephant in the room, the true reason that THE TRIAL OF PETER PARKER was so widely reviled and hated when it was first released. While all problems I previously mentioned are the comic’s major failings in my opinion, the one criticism that has overshadowed all of them over the years has been the story’s ending. At the end of the story, Peter, Ben, and MJ are called to the lab of Dr. Seward Trainer, Ben’s friend and confidant. Dr. Trainer there provides evidence that Peter may actually be the clone instead of Ben Reilly, and after a series of extensive tests, the results show that Ben is in fact the original Spider-Man, and that Peter has been the clone this whole time. Unsurprisingly, the decision to reveal that Peter was the clone was heavily criticized by both fans and several members of Marvel’s staff, as it implied that the Spidey readers had been following since the 1970’s was not the real one. To be perfectly honest though, what bothers me is not the revelation itself, as I feel that fooling the readers that Peter was actually the clone (albeit temporarily in my opinion) could have been made to work (and I'll explain why in a minute). So for me, it’s not the revelation itself that angers me, but rather how it is portrayed in the story. Immediately after discovering that he’s a clone, Peter erupts into a violent rage and assaults Ben Reilly, nearly killing him and accidentally hitting MJ in a controversial moment before running off in despair (all of which is made worse by Sal Buscema's artwork at its absolute worst; seriously, the amount of scratchy pencils and inks, along with brown and murky colors actually made the issue physically painful to look at when I was reading it). What angers me about the handling of this scene is not only that Peter’s behavior completely contradicts his earlier defenses of Ben as a noble person (he immediately thinks the absolute worst of Ben and Dr. Trainer), but also that the writers treat the idea that Peter being a clone is the absolute worst thing that could ever happen to him. That’s just bullcrap in my opinion, as I feel that it doesn’t really matter whether or not he’s a clone. As I’ve stated in throughout these CLONE SAGA reviews, while both Peter and Ben are both great characters in their own right, with different lives and experiences, they still possess the beliefs and morals that make them Peter Parker at heart. Essentially, they are both Peter Parker, and could easily fill in the role as Spider-Man. It doesn’t matter if one of them is a clone as they are both human beings that are different yet the same. So instead of outright stating that Peter is a clone and that he should be disregarded, I feel that the writers should have instead either left the idea of who was the clone ambiguous, or simply say that it doesn't really matter. That way the readers could accept both Peter and Ben as Spider-Man, while also holding onto the value of Peter’s existence in stories that took place before THE CLONE SAGA. I know that this idea can work because I witnessed it in THE “REAL” CLONE SAGA 6-issue miniseries, where Peter’s words of, “I thought about this long and hard… and I honestly don’t care if I am the clone. The way I live is more important than the way I was born. I want to focus on the future. Not the Past,” truly hit home with me, and showed me just how much potential this idea possessed. But that’s just my opinion on the whole matter…

To say that THE TRIAL OF PETER PARKER is a bad story is an understatement in my opinion. In fact, before I delved into the utter idiocy that was MAXIMUM CLONAGE, I was nearly prepared to call this one the worst of all THE CLONE SAGA stories. Aside from the one good scene that revealed Kaine’s identity as a failed Spider-Man clone, virtually nothing is done right in this story. The narrative structure is filled to the brim with horrendous padding, the dialogue is extremely repetitive, the titular trial is both horribly written and given little focus, half of the book is either fight scenes between Spider-Man and Kaine or the boring pseudo-trial by Judas Traveller, and the final reveal of Peter being the clone, and Ben the original, which could have been done extremely well in my humble opinion, was both horribly mishandled and only managed to piss off long-time Spider-Man fans. When I first decided to start doing this series of CLONE SAGA reviews, I knew from the beginning that THE TRIAL OF PETER PARKER was going to be one of the worst, but I was completely unprepared for just how bad it actually was! Man did I hate this one!!! The only question is… what could possibly be worse than this?! Well find out in part 9 of CLONE MADNESS, where we finally reach the absolute worst of all the CLONE SAGA stories… MAXIMUM CLONAGE!!!

“When reading a book, one hopes it doesn’t turn into a painful process. Predictable is bad enough. Laborious is acceptable if the labor produces fruit. But with painfully bad writing, all one can do is grab a hatchet, slice off its head, and bury it.”
' Chila Woychik, ON BEING A RAT AND OTHER OBSERVATIONS

So I have actually purchased 3 of these heavy duty singer machines in the past month. Yes, THREE in the past month. The first one died after 4 - 5 days of use. Problem sprang up suddenly with the bobbin and the whole thing would not budge or work again. I thought the first one was a lemon so I returned it to Amazon and went out to purchase a new machine.

Sadly everywhere around where I live only sells Singers so got another one hoping for the best. After 5 days of constant use, the same thing happened. I returned this one and now being stuck in a place where I cannot get another brand quick enough I exchanged it for another of the same machine. But after 5 days this one is also now completely broken with the same problem in the bobbin. Now also a piece of metal has come off as well. Total piece of junk.

I'm not a professional seamstress but I am sewing a lot for my job and on all 3 machines I was sewing appx 6 - 8 hrs a day with basic materials - cotton and jersey. Nothing special or heavy duty and never more than 3 layers, most of the time it was just 2 layers. I am so pissed off and frustrated. This is the 3rd time this machine has caused me to fall behind on my deadlines.

All I can say is DO NOT BUY IT. I will never buy another singer but going back to the Brother I had used for the same job for 6 months and never even had a hiccup. I let my mother take it when I got the first crappy Singer (wanted to try it because it was supposed to be fast) but now I'm getting the Brother back from her. It will be a 6 hour drive to retrieve it so thank you Singer for causing me to lose yet another day of my life dealing with your piece of crap machines.

Seriously, DO NOT buy this machine.

I felt I had to come on here and leave a review because having owned 3 of them in the same month (2 different models of the heavy duty Singer, the 4423 and the 4411) I can say with 100% accuracy that these machines are junk and will not last. Even if you just use it a couple hours a week, in about 2 months you'll have to return it or suck up the cost of buying a new one if you can't return it. Total piece of crap and a huge time waster. Singer should be absolutely ashamed and I plan on taking this to social media - they should not be selling this machine or really any machine if this is the junk they produce.

top_profane.text:
There's a reason this kit is sold for fifteen bucks. It's horrid. The fit is crap. The accuracy is crap. The measurements are crap. The decals are crap.

I used more filler putty below the wings where they meet the fuselage than I ever have on any other kit. The gap was almost a quarter inch wide by nearly as deep. The landing gear struts aren't long enough to allow the finished kit to sit on both main gear wheels at the same time. It actually rests on one wheel, or the other, and the keel. The landing gear itself looks like it came from a young kid's toy plane. The crew figures aren't so much 1940s pilots as they are 1930s football players. You can see through the front of the engines, through the wings, and out through the flaps. The decals are poorly manufactured and very brittle, needing easily fifteen minutes to soak and even then they come off the backing in pieces. I used leftovers from a Tamiya Corsair and a Hobby Boss Intruder.

Mid way through the build I decided I'd work it as a restoration, not a build, and that helped my state of mind, but all in all I'd rather have my fifteen bucks back.
top_profane.title: Worst manufactured kit I have ever built!

So that is all of my findings on these pens & on ANY metallic glittery gel pens period! I hope this helps someone!

And P.S. Sorry about the tangents! ;)

top_rant.text:
These arrived over the weekend and I have had some time to try them out and there is only 2 good things I can say so far....... they put down a lot of color without much effort and the range of colors are absolutely beautiful!!!! Now my issues with them....I cannot erase them...I prefer erasable colored pencils sometimes....espically when coloring the postcards (re: Lost Ocean) I used cheap pencils to color the postcards because you have to sharpen often. My second problem is the keep breaking as I am trying to sharpen them...or they even seem like the colors are already broken...so they are very fragile. I am going to attempt to bake them. Preheat oven to 350 degrees, lay tin foil on large cookie sheet let "cook" for maybe 10 minutes then turn off the oven and let sit over night before moving ****CAUTION...I HAVE NOT TRIED THIS SO I HAVE NO IDEA IF THEY MAY CATCH ON FIRE, BURN OR PUT OUT ANY FUMES OR DO ANY OTHER HARMFUL THING SO I TAKE NO RESPONSIBILITY FOR ANY RESULTS YOU MAY HAVE. I ONLY SHARE THIS BECAUSE IT MAKES SENSE TO ME IT MAY WORK TO HEAL THE CORES IN THE PENCIL****** If can get back on I will try to remember to publish my results. I intend to do the same with my Prismacolor Premier pencils.

I want to add to this...the seller sent me another set via USPS. I did sharpen a few to ultra sharp points.....they sharpened nicely....so I assume it was the way the first set was shipped? This is beyond the sellers control. I still intend to try to cook the first set to see if I can get the cores to heal. The thing I LOVE about this set is how many colors there are.....so close in range you can get perfect mixes. Thank you very much seller for replacing the first set!!!!!! Okay I know the man who sells these pencils wants an honest opinion. This started out with me objecting the not so biased review....so here are my real thoughts. I just got done going thru all 240 pencils in the 2 sets. Out of all I had to sharpen maybe 18 or so. Some of the pencils are not sharpened enough but it is no big deal. While I was re-sharpening them I found 1 more that is breaking every time I sharpen....that makes a total of 2. So my plans are to also go thru my Prismacolor Premiers to see which of them need to also be cooked. I want to do this all at one time because I think there may be fumes when I do. Also out of 240 of these pencils I found 1 that was split down the length of the wood. I tossed it in the trash. These pencils are very inexpensive so it was no biggie. I would be very careful not to drop them....the cores are fragile....just as the much more expensive Prismacolors are. What I LOVE about these pencils....honestly I was very happy to find out there was problems with only 3 of the pencils because the color range on these is FABULOUS!!!!! I have never seen a set with such a good range. Honestly I do wish the manufacturer of a very expensive set would expand their range. Now while they are impossible to erase the color goes down BEAUTIFUL!!!!! I actually prefer these over my Prismacolor. The don't lie down a thick layer of wax as the Prismacolor tend to. These pencils can be difficult to blend but the Prismacolor colorless blender pencil does help. Also right now I am on a postcard kick.....coloring far more postcards now. When you color the post cards you have to sharpen often. I have no problem having to sharpen them because of their low price. I refuse to use any expensive pencils on the post cards. These pencils are not named or numbered for color. That does not matter to me. I keep a sheet of printer paper on my clipboard behind what I am.working on to check color and test blends. I was making charts and realized it was a waste of time. In the future I think the only thing I will chart is watercolors (not watercolor pencils). I have to say all in all I am VERY happy with these pencils!!!!! They are a fabulous bargain....you just have to treat them carefully...but you also have to do that with Prismacolor and the cost appx 4 times as much. So in the end I can honestly say I take back my original negative remarks and at this price I may buy 1 or 2 more sets of these in the near future to put away to use as replacements....keep in mind I have at least 10 or 12 other sets of inexpensive pencils (I have been buying allot of inexpensive sets) and these pencils are near the top along with 1 more set. Should you buy these YES!!! After I get the 2 pencils cooked I will report how that went and I will also be adding a link to an excellent pencil sharpener when I get on my laptop. The $6 sharpener pays for its self in about an hour of coloring....it does not eat away as much of the casing of the pencil.******** More comments.... When the man who sells these seen how unhappy I was he was kind enough to send me another set. I have to say I jumped too quickly to give a negative review. I am a professional artist (acrylic painter) and know from experience you usually get what you pay for with art supplies. The chemicals used to make pigments can be very expensive...and that is why you will see lets say a tiny tube of oil or watercolor paint and the price seems outrageous....but usually you are paying for what you get. I have to say these pencils are an exception to that rule!!!!! I mentioned earlier they don't erase...I was wrong. I think I did not do a fair test. I used a dk plum and a dk red pencil to test erasing them. Now I have been working with these pencils all night (about 9 hours) on a piece and I am sooooo pleased!!!!! Yes most of these will erase....that is important to me so I am very glad I was wrong the first time around. Some of the pencils are harder then others....it seems maybe the light colors are harder....but that's not 100% true. I know I used a yellow that went on the paper beautifully. I will have this peice done tonight or tomorrow so as soon as I am I will post a pic here.It is a floral peice and the colors are just fantastic!!!!! I tend to be very heavy handed and I have to force myself to lighten up. I can't say these pencils break any more then any other pencil., I can say they have allot of pigment for inexpensive pencils. Yeah....some of the cases do crack and some of the corres are fragile. I still have not gotten to bake the pencils yet. I have to wait till I have the air conditioning off because I am sure there will be some fumes. I LOVE how many colors there are....it means less blending...oh and speaking of blending they do blend nicely. I either use the lighter of the colors I'm working with or use my Prismacolor colorless blender. Both work just fine. I have allot of brands of pencils from the very inexpensive $2 pencils I got at Walmart to the Faber Castell Polychromos. All the pencils are different and I do believe they all have their place. IMHO these pencils are good for large pages. I have really fallen in love with coloring post cards. Since they are so small the pencils have to be sharpened allot. The price on these pencils allows you to do that without burning thru a $4 pencil in one day. Also most of them sharpen very well. As with any colored pencils you have to be careful not to torque the pencil while in the sharpener. The only thing I can complain about and this is not a big thing is they have no names or numbers. I stopped making color charts. I found it to be a waste of time. I use a piece of scrap paper behind the page I am working on and that way I can test my pencil on that to make sure i have the right color and to see if the blend is going to work well. So Yeah when I got some cash I am going to order a few more sets of these and stash them away,I do have a sharpener I recomend...this will save your pencils and mine paid for itself the first day I had it...it is called "Koh-I-Noor Pencil Sharpener 3-length sharpener" and is sold on Amazon. Anyhow I am VERY, VERY happy with these pencils!!!!!! I had to add this pic. I worked on this for 1 1/2 days. I HATE it...but it's only because it's overwhelming me...my fault. I have to learn to have a lighter hand....but I had to show y'all how much color these pencils can lay down. I had few problems with breaking the core so I was not bearing down that hard. I hope this helps you decide if you want these pencils.
top_rant.title: BOY!!!!! Have I changed my mind....NICE pencils!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!

top_rant.text:
Written a few days ago -

I usually don't write reviews unless I'm especially impressed or disappointed in a product. Or if I'm bored. This is the first time I'm beginning my review before I even put it to use. Wanting to capture the tickle. 😄
Upon opening the box, I actually said "Wow!" out loud, surprising even myself. I was impressed with the number of items in this kit, especially the good quality of it all. The"extras"/bonus items are usually cheap &/or utter junk. The gloves being the absolute worst, consistently, across the board. Took a few shots to capture it all. I hadn't paid much attn to the description when I ordered it, thus my surprise.
1st up - A large 250 ml silicone cup, 2-100 ml silicone cups - both made of diff, yet same(?), silicones. They seem to maybe be a little thicker than the usual ones. The 100ml shape/markings are the same. The 250ml is in a shape designed for squeezing in the middle to control the speed/Amy of the pour. My 1st time with that design. Also, a silicone brush, a pair of...
You know, pretty decent tweezers actually!👍 They're pretty good quality. Not like the pieces of crap made out of tin that you normally get. I would actually use these. 😯 Lol
There were 4 plastic - not silicone, plastic - stir sticks in what looks like a nice, easy to use shape. Using the popcycle sticks (oh! Ya, those would truly close up this kit nicely! *hint...hint😉) or the arrows can be a struggle depending on the amount/cup used thanks to my arthritis. These stir sticks, I would use use with a pour larger than 4-5oz. The handle is nicely shaped for finger gripping. The rounded edge - best used with silicone cups vs plastic/hard cups. Made of sturdy, what looks like, good quality material.
Next up is a silicone mat, which I have yet to unroll so I'm not sure how large it is. But I'm thinking it's pretty large. And looks like a decent size thickness. There are a few sheets of sand paper. I'm guessing these are different sizes - I haven't opened them yet. Oddly, what looks like cake frosting decorator shapers? I'm not quite sure how this is used with resin, but hey I'll take them. 🤷‍♀️ A bag of 20-30(?) pipettes (which I never figured out the benefit of using, so don't) with some blue gloves (which I hope actually have a shape to them🤞🙏 vs the useless, planet killing, mini garbage bags pretty much every other company sends out with their products - srsly... just don't even bother🙄😒).
And finally, the outside box says it's a 16 oz kit. When you open it - you actually have TWO(2!) 16 oz bottles! Woot! The outside matches the inside! (It took me so long to grasp that 16 oz actually means 2- 8 oz bottles, etc🙄). The bottles have a long, skinny neck, with small openings. Like an old soda bottle. Smooth, no grip bumps. It's been a few minutes since I used resin from this shape container, so I'm not sure what to expect.
So upon opening the box - prior to use - I'm impressed with the initial presentation & surprising # of items included in the kit. (Whilst in the middle of a bad day even! This was my ray of sunshine 😄) I was so geeked, I wanted to capture the pleasant surprise before the good feelings wore off. 😄. Lol
This would be absolutely fantastic for a beginner, IMO. It contains pretty much all the basic tools needed (short - the small stir sticks for small pours)! 🤗 One would just have to purchase the molds, or the cups, or whatever is being used with the resin.
Oh! I mustn't forget 🤦‍♀️ - there were a total of 20 little measuring cups made of plastic. 10-10 ml & an additional 10 were 20 ml. Plus, 2 squeeze bottle tips with screw on caps - approx 1.5in +/- on diameter. See? There was so much stuff, I forgot some items. Lol
Hopefully it plays nice with other resins🙏 as I'm the middle of a project and I ran out of a different brand. So I will find that out next.
One thing that was different in a way - upon opening the box there was a smell that kind of lingered in the air. I'm not sure what the smell is connected to? If it's the silicone or the plastic or the actual resin?🤷‍♀️. I shall find out shortly. It's not sickening, but not something I recall experiencing with any other resin. Tho it does feel/seem plastics, so likely no biggie. - New Car Smell.😉😄
I will update tomorrow with my results from the 1st pour as this will be my last pour for the night. This will be going head to head, in the same item, with another resin that was also new to me (whose review I have yet to write, but gave good results) - in clear mode! 🤗
(The last picture is of another, same size, resin I had purchased at the same time - both being lightning deals. Both being the same size - 1 marked as 32 oz & the other is marked 16oz. Big difference! Someone earned their A in math class!)
Expectations are high & I'm excited to see the results! 🤗

UPDATE (late)-
So I finally got to do a pour. I did a clear pour - a thin layer on an ashtray. I was able to compare it to the other clear layers I had done with a different resin. From what I can tell with the 1/8-1/4 inch thickness of the layer, it appears as though this is the clearer of the two. Which was surprising because when I first did the mix, it was rather bubbly. Concerningly so. However I let it sit for a couple minutes as I've had the experience that that helps dispell the bubbles with a quick swirl of the stick - the bubbles just evaporate.
However that was not the case this time. The bubbles remained. But with doing my thin layer, I was successful in eliminating nearly all the bubbles through the pour & the final ones with the torch.
I also finished off a second project which used mica and glitter and nothing spectacular to report there. It did what it was supposed to do.
I had mentioned the long neck and the small opening previously. That was not an issue & didn't make a difference. It was easier as I did not have to cut a notch to control the flow so that's actually a bonus.
When I get time I will try to do a clear to clear comparison in the same mold, going head to head with another resin that I have on hand.

With the tools that were provided, the large silicone cup? The idea of it, I got it and I liked it. But the actual put into use part of it was rather awkward. The sides where it folds, the silicone is thinner to allow for a clearer view of the level points which are imprinted and how much resin you have in there. I had to switch out my stir stick three times? Maybe four?
I did a 6 oz pour and the large stir stick they provided was just entirely too large. The thinner sides of the large silicone cup just made it skip along the sides as I tried to scrape. I don't know how to articulate it , I just I couldn't use it. While it is a great looking and made item, I don't foresee using it with resin.
It would be best used for larger pours, mixing large ants of paint, baking, etc. The blade of the stick is more than half the interior diameter of a small silicone cup and about 1/3 the diameter of a normal version of the large silicone cup. I haven't tried it in the normal style cup so I don't know if it'll work better there.
The large silicone cup with a thinner sections in the side also make it awkward for using any stirrer. I found a large popsicle stick being the best tool, but even that I wasn't really fond of the way it worked. So I'll probably use that cup for other purposes. That was a disappointment as I really liked the idea of the design. I discovered in use - it's not practical. The smaller silicone cups work as expected perfectly.
I don't use pipettes as I've never understood the benefit of them. I did get to use the 20 oz mixing cup to fill in little spots here and there. I used my small arrow skinny stir stick for that.
The gloves, despite the fingers being a bit too long (which does affect the pour), were great. They seem like hospital gloves. They are not too tight, just a bit too loose - but 100x better than the hand garbage bags sent in other kits. You can also use your phone touch screen with them on😁. Though I do not recommend that if you have resin on your fingers.
Everything was able to be cleaned with alcohol wipe with no problems.
I did not use the sandpaper, squeeze bottle tops. I would love instructions on how to utilize the sandpaper,in what order, etc. In legible English. With pics would be great (I don't watch videos). I never seem to acquire the results promised.
I'm not quite ready to unroll the silicone mat yet. I have cats whose hair clings to anything with a molecule, so I am saving that for when It's desperately needed. Nor have I used the squeeze bottle tops and don't really see myself doing so. At least not for the resin. There may come a time for using it for something else.

I didn't really pay attention to the cure time as I did the pour at night and didn't tend to it again until the following afternoon. I'll try to make my next pour in the morning so I can check it through the day.
However, pouring clear, was crystal CLEAR. Not foggy clear.👍 I do suggest, as with any other resin, to do a clear pore in small layers. I pour about a millimeter depth, then torch or heat - to remove the bubbles. Add another millimeter, - rinse and repeat. If you just pour a deep amount of the clear, there will always be bubbles that you cannot get to. It doesn't take that much extra time on the results are well worth it.

This is a great kit with a wide range of tools provided for the many different styles and ways of using the resin. It gives one ability to experiment and play.
I will restate that I highly recommend this product - especially for beginners or first timers. You get most everything in one kit and then some. It's all high quality. It may spoil you, causing you great disappointment when you order a different brand and see the low quality, "bonus" items they send out.
I also strongly suggest adding a handful of popsicles sticks. The large stir stick provided just does not work with these cups. It is likely best used with larger containers/larger pours. If you add 5 or 6 popsicle sticks, users can at least get a couple of pours out of them, even in the teeny tiny cups, to get a feel for what additional items they need to purchase.
This is a starter kit for a reason. To start one out on this journey with resin. This item provides almost everything needed to help first timer/beginners & new users decide if this is something they really want to get into.
Side note - I developed a chemical burn in Nov from a brand that I had previously used for almost a year. A smudge or 2 on my palm & fingers was all it took. Then 3+mos to heal. I've since confirmed that it was that particular batch, from that particular brand, that caused the burn versus a general development of sensitivity to resin. I have used this & another brand of resin with no problems. However, I still strongly recommend using full gloves versus the finger rubbers because you never know which batch will cause harm to you. It took less than 24 hours for the initial rash to develop on my hand with no warning. So prevention is very important. This is a warning from someone who spits in the wind and does as she wishes versus following instructions.

I will absolutely be keeping my out eye out for this on sales & would recommend it to anyone - beginner or expert - as in my experience this is a good quality product that gives you amazing results. When used according to direction.
top_rant.title: Highly recommend

top_rant.text:
I've been doing basic machine sewing and hand-embroidery for years, but this is my first time trying an embroidery machine. So, this review is from a beginner's perspective, without any previous machines for comparison.

The good news is, for beginners, this may look intimidating when you pull it out of the box but is relatively easy to set up! If you've never used a horizontally-set upper thread spool, that may be a small learning curve; it's awkward at first but not complicated. Because the awesome little bonus-pack included some pre-wound bobbins, I skipped over the bobbin winding instructions for now. I did not know that machine embroidery requires a different type of bobbin thread! It includes a larger spool of white bobbin thread to get you started, even without the pre-wound ones. Threading the bobbin is a piece of cake, just like my regular Brother it's a quick drop-in - just make sure the thread is going the right direction (there are diagrams on the machine, don't worry!). The Quick Reference Guide for Upper Threading is pretty confusing though, because it begins step 1 with connecting the carriage, turning it on, opening the cover, etc. So, by the time you get to actually threading the machine, the book is #10 while the corresponding numbers on the machine start with #1. Not insurmountable, but a little confusing. It has a few more steps than threading a regular sewing machine, but it's not very different.
- - - -
EDITED FROM ORIGINAL REVIEW: It took me quite a while to figure out the automatic threader, I broke a lot of threads and a couple of nails trying to get it to work. After about 30 little projects (with multiple colors of thread in each), I think I've got it! You have to make sure the thread has securely gone through the little "snap" next to the needle to hold it in place, thread it around the side (there's a diagram) and hold it LOOSELY as you pull the lever. I don't have good luck with the little thread cutter on the side, that seems to hold it too tightly for effective threading. It needs enough slack to push the thread through the needle eye, then you can manually pull it the rest of the way through. For some reason it seems to work better for me with the presser foot in the UP position - don't know why because this doesn't affect needle position, but it works for me. Now back to your regularly scheduled review!
- - - -
Assembling the stabilizer & fabric into a hoop is easy and the instructions are clear. Please note that the bonus set includes extra hoops, but has stabilizer sheets for the 1x2.5 and 4x4 hoop ONLY, it does not include stabilizer for the 5x7 hoop, so you'll want to pick some up (cut sheets or a roll) before testing that hoop out. The hoops fasten securely to the carriage, though they require a little maneuvering to get them under the presser foot. Once you've got your fabric & hoop set up, your needle & bobbin threaded, the little LED screen takes over! It's very easy to navigate, pretty much walks you through the process - I had no idea you could customize the design position and size so much! It has an option to check on what colors are needed before you start, and shows you which parts of the design are used for each color. Maybe this is normal for embroidery machines but I liked it! I'm a little rebel when it comes to color charts, so I like to know which parts I can swap for a different shade. The pixelated designs are nearly impossible to see on the tiny screen, so you'll want to use the included book for reference. Choose a design and hit go! Watch the design magically appear!

Trouble-shooting: the screen actually tells you what is wrong, for the most part, which is REALLY nice. I often don't realize that my presser foot is in the wrong position but it tells me what to do before moving the carriage. I had one project where I broke a needle (my fault - totally inappropriate fabric choice) and it stopped immediately and beeped for help. I was able to change the needle and rethread it, hit the button and it was off and running again, mid-design. I also had some thread jam up and break (same fabric, haha) and same thing: it stopped work and let me fix it before continuing. It wound up missing a few stitches on the final design, but it actually didn't look bad and I was very impressed! One thing it did NOT tell me was when I had the machine too close to the wall, rookie mistake! It continued working away, just overlapping the stitch area where it had wanted to move the hoop around but bumped into the wall instead. So, learn from me and scoot that guy away from the wall! I also screwed up and layered my fabric under the hoop once; thankfully just a scrap for testing. It chowed right through it like a champ. Bad news for that fabric, but good news for thicker projects!

It comes with over 100 pre-installed small embroidery motifs, including several alphabet styles. Most of them are pretty basic, granny-looking stuff - lottttts of flowers. The *bonus* pack with this machine includes a CD-ROM of even more designs, but mostly the same kind of thing: cute, but I don't want to make anything with them. I wanted to try anyway to see how transferring designs via the USB port would work, and after a little confusion, it worked great! I don't know if it's because I'm on a Mac, but I can't see any of the designs or even the PDF on my computer screen. Fortunately, the PDF printed fine and included thumbnails of every design on the CD, with a corresponding number so they're easy to look up. Click and drag the ones you want onto your USB stick, move it over to the embroidery machine, follow the instructions (super easy, mostly on-screen), and off you go! I also tried downloading a few designs off the internet to see if that would work. It can read either .DFT or .PES files, though .PES files give you a little pixel-preview on the LED screen and .DFT just shows you a file name. I still can't see a preview of any designs on my laptop after downloading, but the click-and-drag method works with the USB regardless! Works so well, in fact, that I downloaded many many new designs on my first night and have already used several! Renaming the files doesn't affect their functionality, so I renamed them all to file them by description.

The bonus pack also includes 6 spools of basic colors: black, white, red, green, black, and yellow. You'll need a lot more for most designs but this will get you started! Other than the threader issue, the included thread worked perfectly and smoothly. I also had a few other brands of embroidery thread on hand from a recent shopping spree (I had no embroidery machine at the time but they were pretty! don't judge!) and they worked perfectly too! The only breakage was due to a weird fabric I decided to play with (extremely tight weave); the thread was not at fault and neither was the machine. The machine itself comes with one hoop, one spool of bobbin thread, and all the other little necessities that every machine includes for operation (1 bobbin, little brush, mini screwdriver, etc). The bonus pack includes 2 more hoops, 2 sizes of stabilizer, the 6-pack of thread, a package of pre-wound bobbins AND a bag of empty bobbins, a pack of needles, the CD-ROM of extra designs, and a little curved snipper that REALLY helps you get up close to the design to snip off any stray threads! LOVE those snippers! Oh, and more good news: it uses regular standard needles and the same basic bobbins that all my other Brother machines have used, so no extra expense on that front!

Maybe I'm simply dazzled because I've never had an embroidery machine before, but the things this toy can do are absolutely amazing to me! I'm very impressed at how easy it was for a beginner to get started - I went from opening the box to making little red & green cherries on cotton in less than 45 minutes. It has done everything I asked it to do, from downloading designs to integrating metallic thread. I made a few silly rookie mistakes but they were easy to fix and the machine helped me along. I have the sense that I've barely scratched the surface of the incredible things this machine will do, and the fact that getting started was so easy makes me excited to learn more. I almost deducted 1 star for the automatic-threader problem, but I'm very glad I didn't because that turned out to be user error too! I LOVE IT. It is my new boyfriend and we are going to live happily ever after together!
top_rant.title: My First Embroidery Machine, and I Am In Love

top_rant.text:
UPDATE: In the text below with all I have to say...I want to stress this as well: Regarding the two screen tightening bolts that were shorted us(7/16 by 3 1/2" plastic knob capped bolts)so that we could not complete this press: I contacted seller(Lars), who in several contacts(we even sent PICTURES OF!!) chose to play dumb and act as if he did not understand what we were needing. We even responded to the email(copy paste from amazon)email he sent REQUESTING such pics...and he said he "did not receive". He HAS pics of the very product itself on his own amazon page, and the parts we need are CLEARLY discernible. It has been NINE days since we received this 'partial press' and it seems to be as much work to get seller to wake up and even KNOW his product, as it turn out to fix all the other issues with. He is assuming I will join him in his pretense of ignorance. I will NOT. I am making and will soon post everythinjg I have to share about this equipment, at my YouTube channel (TeeLogic) . Be sure to watch BEFORE you buy this gear!!!! If you already bought it...watch the videos to fix(correctly)any of the issues that can and do plague this budget-import-poorly mfg. and packaged gear. There are some good things about it...but NOT enough for anyone beyond the kindergarten level of screen printing. If you want quality multicolor printing(with any close registration)...FORGET IT. However...my videos are also about an upgrade you can easily add that can turn this gear into an amazingly accurate press. Your call. I would not(could not)use this press (dependably!)without such micro tuning ADDED. The ONLY reason we will keep this gear is because of the changes I was able to effect (micro-tuning and other important mods). As far as the equipment in it's own right(what comes in...or is missing from!...'the box')is concerned...we are NOT impressed with it, NOR the games as we tried to get the simple parts we need. I originally gave this a three star rating(being nice)...but now that I am left to chase down specialty parts that SHOULD HAVE BEEN PROVIDED to us; at added expense to me as well....I have to back that rating off to a ONE(Aweful!)star.
Buying this gear is like 'going to Vegas'...You might win or lose...a little of a lot. YOUR CALL. Just do not expect the NON-screenprinters selling it to be of much or any help to you. it is cheaper for them to just make excuses as to why they cannot help. I know better...and i wrote all of this(long rant!) to make sure YOU do as well. I would LOVE to have said only positive things...but the experience does not warrant undue praise. I have been a screen printer; equipment designer/builder and worked in EVERY phase (extensively) of screen printing, since 1979. I designed the best manual screen printing micro tuning system in the world(1990). And THIS imported 'wanna-be' screen printing (looks like, anyways)...NEEDS IT as much or more than any gear I have ever seen/used. Don't let the low cost fool you. a press either works accurate/exact/correct or it does not. This equipment did not and could not as it comes. Now it lines up AWESOME.
As ironic as that sounds! MY attention to necessary details(NOT the mfg. who push this stuff from quick profit motives alone)as a true screen printer...IS the reason why. I hate to think of how many new screen printers, do not know of or will be able to fix, what they should, BEFORE buying this gear. Even the platen design(watch my videos) makes NO sense. You would not see platens of that dimension, even onlarger industrial ro-shop presses. They are exagerated on this equipment(to get your attention?). I made my own of the CORRECT dimensions. Please read on!

This 4 color two station 'Maxwolf' screen printing press was purchased on team memeber Susan Robert's account. So on that name/account is where the feedback must be left. However, i am Lee Wyke- team member and pro screen printer(of anything and everything)for over 30 years; I am the assembler of the equipment. This equipment was purchased, mainly, for the purpose of review, to see if it was in and of itself, worthy of being used as a platform for the micro-tuning system I invented back in 1990. It appeared to me before our purchase of this equipment; based on user comments and customer approval ratings, that this equipment has; does and is likely to continue attracting a large number of would-be screen printers, who cannot see nor cannot be expected to understand(as new printers) the 'for better or worse' that this equipment demonstrates in it's true form. As a real and experienced screen printer, I wanted to realize myself, just what was 'in the box, and how little or much needed to be fixed/adjusted, before a person could actually move forward on this equipment...meaning: convert it into an ACCURATE multi-color press, that could rise to even the basic dependability of operation, that a savvy screen printer would expect, and that even a new screen printer should get for their money. A press(at sub 200 dollars, or a press at 2,000 or more dollars(seen many)that does not perform it's primary function corectly...is worthless IMO. So...I received thepress; built it, and video detailed every issue and real world concern i have regarding it.
